name: Crusty Crustacean Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Place warm water in a large bowl and sprinkle yeast over it. Stir until dissolved.
2. Add milk, sugar, melted butter, and salt to the yeast mixture. Gradually add flour, mixing with a wooden spoon until well combined.
3. Beat eggs lightly and mix into the dough with enough flour to form a soft dough. Turn onto a floured surface and knead for 4-6 minutes until smooth and elastic.
4. Place in a greased bowl, turning to coat all sides. Cover with a towel and let rise at room temperature until doubled, about 30-45 minutes.
5. Punch down the dough and remove it to a lightly floured board. Divide into two parts; set one aside for the body and divide the other into twelve equal pieces.
6. Use eight pieces to form crab legs. Combine two pieces to make each claw. Place the large piece onto a greased baking sheet and shape into a crab body. Attach legs and claws.
7. Use raisins for eyes and lemon strips as antenna. Cover and let rise in a warm spot until doubled, about 20-40 minutes.
8. Preheat oven to 400°F. Beat remaining egg lightly and brush over the bread surface.
9. Bake for 10-15 minutes or until done. Check after 10 minutes; if dark, cover with foil. Remove from baking sheet and let cool on a rack.
Makes 1 large loaf suitable for serving as a centerpiece.
